etymology.description: 特(特) (tè, "special") + 殊(殊) (shū, "different") combine to express the notion of being out of the ordinary or distinguished.
etymology.interpretation: 特(特) (tè, "special") refers to something that stands out or is not common, suggesting singularity or uniqueness. 殊(殊) (shū, "different") stresses a divergence from the norm or being distinct. Together, the word 特殊 (tèshū, "special") synthesizes these ideas to convey the sense of being exceptional or notably different, emphasizing both distinctiveness and particularity.

examples.chinese: 这份礼物很特殊(這份禮物很特殊)。
他有一个特殊的才能(他有一個特殊的才能)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī gè tèshū de cáinéng.

examples.chinese: 在特殊情况下(在特殊情況下)，我们需要做出调整(我們需要做出調整)。
examples.pinyin: Zài tèshū qíngkuàng xià, wǒmen xūyào zuòchū tiáozhěng.
examples.english: In special circumstances, we need to make adjustments.
